summary: Smoke regression after Railway-readiness backend changes. Backend startup log shows 'Startup complete: indexes ensured and seeds run.' Both new health endpoints behave correctly via preview proxy: /api/health -> 200 {status:ok,db:ok}. /health (no /api) is shadowed by the preview's frontend proxy and returns the SPA HTML (200) — this is a preview-domain proxy behavior; on Railway it will hit the backend directly. Auth, admin dashboard, CSV export, role guard, and CORS preflight all pass. UI: admin lands on /admin and Team Overview fully renders (charts + totals); employee maya lands on /tasks and Kanban populates with 5 tasks (3 in-progress, 2 done).
passed: Supervisor startup log: 'Startup complete: indexes ensured and seeds run.'
CORS preflight OPTIONS /api/auth/login -> 204 with access-control-allow-origin:*, allow-headers:*, allow-methods incl POST
UI admin login -> /admin renders Team Overview, Workload chart, Status breakdown, employee table (no infinite spinner)
UI employee maya login -> /tasks renders Kanban (To Do/In Progress/Done) populated with seed tasks

failed.issue: GET /health (no /api prefix) on the preview domain returns the React SPA HTML (200) because the preview ingress proxies only /api/* to the backend. On Railway (single-service deploy of backend) the new /health endpoint will be reachable directly. Could not verify Railway healthcheck through preview URL — relies on Railway domain.

action_items: No code changes required for preview. When deploying to Railway, verify Railway healthcheck path is /health (or switch to /api/health which is also available and pings DB).
